reason: Constant arithmetic is literally false as written. The chain reads: delta=||P-Phi||<=||S|| ||R-I||/2<=3*(C eta)/2=C eta. Substituting the established bounds ||S||<=3 (node 1.1) and ||R-I||<=C eta (node 1.2, where C is DEFINED as C=4 C(eta_0)) gives delta<=(1/2)*3*(C eta)=(3/2) C eta. The asserted equality 3*(C eta)/2 = C eta is (3/2) C eta = C eta, which is false (off by a factor 3/2) unless C=0. The single symbol C is used simultaneously as node 1.2's fixed input constant and as the node's output constant within ONE equation chain, so the equality cannot be repaired by the 'universal constant grows line-to-line' convention without an explicit relabel. Correct form: delta<=(3/2) C eta =: C' eta with C':=(3/2)*4 C(eta_0)=6 C(eta_0) a NEW named constant; then ||P||<=1+(3/2)C eta=1+C' eta. As written, the equation states a false numerical identity.
